name: profile
title: Profile
help: Capture profile: a sensible parameter bundle applied for options you did not set explicitly (your explicit choices always win). 'drone-3d': aerial survey (spatial matching, balanced resolution). 'object': finds/artefacts/turntable (exhaustive matching, full-resolution densify, finest refine, local frame). 'architecture': buildings/facades (exhaustive matching, balanced resolution). 'none': defaults only. Default: %(default)s

name: sparse-engine
title: Sparse Engine
help: Structure-from-Motion backend for the sparse reconstruction. 'colmap' handles both close-range / convergent imagery (objects, architecture) and aerial / GPS sets (with EXIF-GPS or GCP georeferencing and split-merge tiling for large counts). An OpenSfM backend for very large GPS-only nadir missions is planned but not yet available. Default: %(default)s

name: matcher
title: COLMAP Matcher
help: Feature matching strategy (COLMAP only). 'exhaustive' is most robust for unordered object photos but scales O(n^2). 'sequential' for ordered/video frames. 'vocab_tree' scales to large sets (hundreds–thousands of images): each image retrieves its most similar images from a baked-in Flickr100K vocabulary tree instead of matching all pairs. 'spatial' uses GPS when present. Default: %(default)s

name: mapper
title: COLMAP Mapper
help: Sparse SfM strategy (COLMAP only). 'incremental' is the robust default for close-range / convergent sets (objects, architecture). 'global' uses COLMAP 4's built-in GLOMAP global mapper — much faster on large, well-connected (e.g. aerial) blocks, but less robust on convergent close-range imagery; prefer it for big sets / ODM-comparison runs, not as the default. Default: %(default)s

name: camera-model
title: Camera Model
help: COLMAP camera model used for self-calibration. FULL_OPENCV for high-distortion / macro lenses. Default: %(default)s

name: densify-resolution-level
title: Densify Resolution Level
help: OpenMVS DensifyPointCloud downscale level. 0 = full resolution (slowest, densest). Default: %(default)s

name: number-views-fuse
title: Number Views Fuse
help: Minimum number of views that must agree to fuse a 3D point. Higher = cleaner but sparser. ODM uses 2; 3-4 improves close-range quality. Default: %(default)s

name: skip-reconstruct-mesh
title: Skip Mesh Reconstruction
help: Skip OpenMVS ReconstructMesh/RefineMesh/TextureMesh and stop after the dense point cloud. The full mesh chain is the point of this engine and runs by default; check only when you need a cloud-only fast run. Default: %(default)s

name: refine-mesh-iters
title: Refine Mesh Iterations
help: OpenMVS RefineMesh multi-scale refinement iterations (passed to RefineMesh --scales: photometric mesh optimisation against the source images, repeated over image scales). 0 disables RefineMesh entirely. This is the main quality lever vs. commercial tools. Default: %(default)s

name: refine-max-face-area
title: Refine: Max Face Area (px)
help: RefineMesh subdivision threshold: maximum projected face area in pixels before a triangle is split. Smaller = finer mesh and more detail recovered, at higher cost (try 8 for maximum detail, 32 for speed). Default: %(default)s

name: refine-gradient-step
title: Refine: Gradient Step
help: RefineMesh gradient descent step size. Larger = more aggressive surface movement per iteration (risk of artefacts), smaller = conservative refinement. Default: %(default)s

name: cpu-threads
title: CPU Threads (SIFT/Match)
help: Worker threads for COLMAP CPU feature extraction/matching. Capped low by default because the CPU SIFT extractor spikes RAM per thread (OOM risk on large images); raise on hosts with plenty of RAM. Ignored on GPU. Default: %(default)s

name: cpu-match-block
title: CPU Matcher Block Size
help: COLMAP exhaustive-matcher block size on the CPU path. The default FLANN matcher segfaults at large blocks on some hosts (arm64); 10 is safe. Raise cautiously for speed on x86. Ignored on GPU. Default: %(default)s

name: dense-max-threads
title: Dense Stage Max Threads
help: Cap the worker threads of the OpenMVS dense binaries (DensifyPointCloud / ReconstructMesh / RefineMesh / TextureMesh). Each thread holds image pyramids and working buffers, so on many-core but RAM-constrained hosts the per-thread peak can OOM during densify/refine — the same reason 'cpu-threads' is capped for COLMAP's CPU SIFT. 0 (default) = all cores (OpenMVS default, unchanged behaviour). NOTE: this bounds the densify/refine peak only; it does NOT reduce the ReconstructMesh Delaunay tetrahedralization, which is strictly in-core — for that memory wall use 'tiles' (split-merge) or a higher 'densify-resolution-level'. The EFFIGIES_DENSE_THREADS env var overrides it. Default: %(default)s

name: free-space-support
title: Mesh: Free-Space Support
help: OpenMVS ReconstructMesh --free-space-support: use visibility rays to recover weakly-supported surfaces (building walls seen only at grazing angles in nadir flights, textureless areas). Off by OpenMVS default; enable for facades. Default: %(default)s

name: mesh-close-holes
title: Mesh: Close Holes (faces)
help: OpenMVS ReconstructMesh --close-holes: maximum hole size (in faces) to close in the reconstructed mesh. The default 30 closes only small gaps; raise (e.g. 300) to bridge missing facades Metashape-style — bridged areas carry interpolated geometry, not real reconstruction. Default: %(default)s

name: mesh-decimate
title: Mesh Decimation Factor
help: ReconstructMesh decimation. 1.0 = no decimation (full detail). 0.5 = halve faces. Default: %(default)s

name: texture-resolution
title: Texture Resolution
help: TextureMesh output texture atlas size in px. Default: %(default)s

name: skip-color-harmonize
title: Skip Texture Color Harmonization
help: Skip the per-image exposure/colour equalisation before texturing. By default one RGB gain per image (estimated from the sparse-point observations) is applied to the undistorted images, so the texture atlas and orthophoto are assembled from photometrically consistent photos — it replaces OpenMVS seam leveling, which is corrupted on this build. Default: %(default)s

name: skip-view-blending
title: Skip Multi-View Texture Blending
help: Skip the multi-view texture blend: by default every texel is re-baked as a weighted blend of its best source views (view angle and distance weighted, occlusion-tested against mesh depth maps) instead of OpenMVS's single best view — removes per-view exposure/sharpness blotches on homogeneous surfaces (roofs). Default: %(default)s

name: skip-seam-smoothing
title: Skip Texture Seam Smoothing
help: Skip Effigies' own seam leveling: by default, colours are equalised across texture-patch seams (sampled inset from each side, screened-Poisson diffusion into the patch interiors, baked into the atlas) — replaces OpenMVS's corrupted seam leveling. Default: %(default)s

name: texture-seam-leveling
title: Texture Seam Leveling
help: OpenMVS global+local seam leveling (colour harmonisation between views). OFF by default: it corrupts texture patches on this (arm64/CPU) build — black patches with saturated borders — verified broken in BOTH v2.4.0 and master (2026-06). Use the default exposure harmonisation instead (skip-color-harmonize to disable). Default: %(default)s

name: georeference
title: Georeferencing
help: Georeferencing strategy. 'auto': use gcp-file if present, else EXIF-GPS, else fall back to a metrically-scaled local model. 'gcp': require a GCP file. 'exif': require EXIF-GPS. 'none': skip georeferencing entirely and keep a local (object-centric) frame - recommended for turntable / close-range objects. Default: %(default)s

name: crs
title: Target CRS (EPSG)
help: Target projected CRS for georeferencing, given as an EPSG code (e.g. 'EPSG:32633'). 'auto' derives the appropriate UTM zone automatically from the GPS/GCP coordinates. Any projected EPSG code is accepted, including regional grids. Default: %(default)s

name: crs-preset
title: CRS Preset
help: Named regional grid presets that fill Target CRS for you (an explicitly set Target CRS always wins): 'israeli-tm' = EPSG:6991 (Israeli TM Grid), 'palestine-1923' = EPSG:28191 (Palestine 1923 Grid), 'etrs89-utm32n' / 'etrs89-utm33n' = EPSG:25832/25833 — GERMANY's official grid (32N covers western/central Germany incl. NRW and Lower Saxony, 33N the east), 'british-ng' = EPSG:27700 (OSGB National Grid), 'swiss-lv95' = EPSG:2056. 'none' keeps the Target CRS option (default: automatic WGS84 UTM zone). Default: %(default)s

name: gcp
title: GCP File
help: Optional path to a ground control points file (ODM gcp_list.txt format) used for the Helmert georeferencing bridge. Default: none

name: gcp-bundle-adjust
title: GCP-constrained bundle adjustment
help: Anchor the marked GCPs at their surveyed world coordinates in a constrained bundle adjustment (pycolmap / COLMAP's Ceres BA) on the sparse model BEFORE densification, instead of the post-hoc 7-DoF similarity. A rigid similarity cannot absorb reconstruction drift; pulling the GCPs into the BA re-optimises cameras and tie points to be consistent with them, removing drift and tightening the check-point RMSE. 'auto' (default): run both and keep whichever gives the lower INDEPENDENT check-point RMSE - never worse than the post-hoc path on the check metric, and falls back to it when no 'check'-flagged GCPs are present (or there is no GCP file). 'on': always the bundle adjustment. 'off': always the post-hoc similarity. Mark GCPs in 2+ images, and end a gcp_list.txt line in 'check' to hold it out for the accuracy estimate. Default: %(default)s

name: align-to
title: Align to (change detection)
help: Path to a prior epoch's reference point cloud (e.g. another task's odm_georeferencing/odm_georeferenced_model.laz) or a DEM GeoTIFF (a prior DSM/DEM) on the node's data volume, for multi-epoch change detection. When set, this epoch is co-registered to the reference (stable-area-masked PDAL ICP) and difference products are written: a DEM-of-Difference (odm_dem/dem_difference.tif, cut/fill volumes above a minimum level-of-detection) and an M3C2 change cloud (odm_change/m3c2.laz, signed distance + level-of-detection), plus odm_report/change_detection.json. By default this epoch's mesh and cloud - and the DSM/DTM/orthophoto/contours/glTF derived from them - are re-landed into the reference frame so the whole time series overlays (ODM --align parity). Default: none

name: skip-orthophoto
title: Skip Orthophoto
help: Skip generating the orthophoto GeoTIFF. By default a georeferenced orthophoto is nadir-rasterised from the refined textured mesh (odm_orthophoto/odm_orthophoto.tif); it is skipped automatically for non-georeferenced (local-frame) results. Default: %(default)s

name: skip-dsm
title: Skip DSM
help: Skip generating the DSM (digital surface model). By default a georeferenced single-band Float32 GeoTIFF of the topmost surface elevation (odm_dem/dsm.tif) is written from the same nadir rasterisation as the orthophoto — so it inherits the RefineMesh detail at no extra cost; skipped automatically for non-georeferenced (local-frame) results. This is the surface model (buildings/vegetation included); the bare-earth DTM is the 'dtm' option. Default: %(default)s

name: dtm
title: DTM (bare earth)
help: Generate a DTM (digital terrain model): a bare-earth elevation GeoTIFF (odm_dem/dtm.tif) made by PDAL ground classification (SMRF) of the dense point cloud — buildings and vegetation removed, the complement of the DSM. OFF by default: the ground filter costs extra time and a bare-earth model is meaningless for close-range / object captures with no open ground. Requires a georeferenced result. Default: %(default)s

name: classify
title: Classify Point Cloud (ML)
help: ML multi-class classification of the point cloud (OpenPointClass, the same tool ODM uses): tags every point with an ASPRS class — ground, low/medium/high vegetation, building, vehicle — written into the LAZ (so viewers can colour by class), plus class-filtered surface rasters (odm_dem/buildings.tif, odm_dem/canopy.tif). When on, the DTM reuses the ML ground class instead of re-running SMRF. Opt-in: an extra ML step (~2 min per 15M points). Requires a georeferenced result. Default: %(default)s

name: semantic
title: Semantic Orthophoto (v0)
help: Write a per-pixel semantic orthophoto (odm_semantic/orthophoto_semantic.tif) by rasterising the point-cloud classes onto the orthophoto grid (pixel-aligned with the DSM): each pixel gets the dominant coarse class — ground / vegetation / structure — with a colour table and a legend JSON. This is the geometry-derived v0 of the semantic field (no trained model); fine archaeological material classes (stone/earth/paving/ceramic/mortar) are a downstream model. Needs the cloud classified, so enable Classify too; self-skips otherwise. Default: %(default)s

name: orthophoto-resolution
title: Orthophoto Resolution (cm/px)
help: Orthophoto ground sample distance in cm/pixel. 'auto' targets ~4k px across the scene (clamped 1 cm .. 1 m). Smaller = sharper but larger file. Default: %(default)s

name: ortho-fill-holes
title: Orthophoto: Fill Small Holes (m²)
help: Maximum hole area (in m²) filled in the orthophoto with the nearest valid colour. Only small INTERIOR holes below this size are closed (pinholes, thin triangle seams, tiny mesh gaps); large voids (e.g. missing walls) and the outer boundary stay transparent (honest nodata). 0 disables filling. Affects only the visual orthophoto — the DSM, DTM and point cloud are never modified. Default: %(default)s

name: ortho-color-balance
title: Orthophoto: Colour Balance
help: Radiometric finishing of the FINAL orthophoto raster (the single-mesh ortho has no seams, so there is nothing to seamline-edit; this is the colour-balance half of Metashape/ODM ortho finishing). 'none' (default): output unchanged. 'white-balance': gray-world cast removal (per-channel gain so neutral surfaces read neutral). 'auto': white-balance plus a 1–99 percentile luminance contrast stretch. Colour consistency between source views is already handled upstream at the texture atlas — this only normalises the assembled ortho. A residual-tonal-variation metric is always logged (odm_report/orthophoto_finishing.json) so you can tell whether balancing is even needed. Default: %(default)s

name: ortho-brightness
title: Orthophoto: Brightness
help: Manual additive brightness applied to the orthophoto, normalised to -1..1 (i.e. ±255 8-bit levels). 0 = off. Applied after colour balance. Default: %(default)s

name: ortho-gamma
title: Orthophoto: Gamma
help: Manual gamma tone curve for the orthophoto. 1.0 = off; >1 darkens mid-tones, <1 lifts them. Applied after colour balance and brightness. Default: %(default)s

name: ortho-flatten
title: Orthophoto: Large-Scale Flatten
help: Strength (0..1) of a large-scale luminance flatten that removes broad, low-frequency brightness gradients across the orthophoto (the Metashape-style radiometric balancing). 0 = off. WARNING: on an excavation ortho a genuine soil-colour / feature gradient IS data — flattening it destroys signal. Leave off unless the residual tonal-variation metric (odm_report/orthophoto_finishing.json) shows a real exposure gradient. Default: %(default)s

name: contours-interval
title: Contour Interval (m)
help: Generate vector contour lines at this spacing in metres (0 disables). Written to odm_dem/contours.gpkg (GeoPackage, for GIS) and odm_dem/contours.dxf (for CAD), as 3D lines at their elevation with an 'elev' attribute. Generated from the DTM if present (true bare-earth terrain contours — enable the DTM option), otherwise the DSM. Needs a georeferenced result. Default: %(default)s

name: 3d-tiles
title: 3D Tiles (Cesium streaming)
help: Export an OGC 3D Tiles tileset (LOD tileset.json + b3dm tiles) of the textured mesh for web/Cesium streaming of large scenes, written to odm_3d_tiles/. Built with OpenDroneMap's Obj2Tiles (splits + decimates the mesh into level-of-detail tiles). Opt-in: it is an extra processing step that emits many files. Needs a georeferenced result. Default: %(default)s

name: no-gpu
title: No GPU
help: Force CPU processing even when a CUDA GPU is available. Without this flag the engine uses the GPU when present and falls back to CPU automatically (with a warning) when not. Default: %(default)s

name: no-auto-scale
title: Disable Auto-Scaling
help: Disable the engine's automatic adaptation to large image sets. By default, for options you did not set explicitly, Effigies adapts to the image count: above ~150 images it switches the COLMAP matcher away from the O(n^2) 'exhaustive' strategy to 'vocab_tree'; above ~500 it also prefers the global (GLOMAP) mapper and bounds full-resolution densify — each decision is logged and any explicit choice always wins. Check this to keep your selected options exactly as set, with no count-based adaptation. Default: %(default)s

name: tiles
title: Split-merge tiling
help: Split the dense reconstruction into spatial tiles to stay within a memory budget on large image sets (the open-source analogue of Metashape chunks / ODM split-merge). SfM runs once on all images; the cameras are partitioned in that one shared frame, only the dense->mesh->texture chain runs per tile, and the results are merged into one set of assets (alignment is free, no GPS needed). 'off' (default): never tile. 'auto': tile only when the estimated dense-point total exceeds the per-tile budget (below it the run is byte-identical to non-tiled). A positive integer N: force exactly N tiles. Tiling is COLMAP-only; the mesh has hairline seams at tile borders (v1 limitation). Default: %(default)s

name: tile-budget
title: Per-tile memory budget (points)
help: Target dense-point count per tile for 'tiles=auto' — the knob that trades tile size against the dense-stage peak RAM (ReconstructMesh's Delaunay is the wall). 'auto' uses the built-in default (or the EFFIGIES_TILE_BUDGET env var); a positive integer sets it explicitly. Larger = fewer, bigger tiles (more RAM); smaller = more tiles (more border seams + overhead). The default is a conservative placeholder pending calibration against measured RAM. Default: %(default)s

name: keep-workdir
title: Keep Workdir Intermediates
help: Keep the engine's intermediate working data (depth maps, undistorted images, mesh snapshots, ~6-8 GB per full-res run) after the task finishes. By default they are cleaned at the end of the run — the delivered assets are unaffected — because a persistent task volume otherwise fills the disk after a few runs. Default: %(default)s
